Oracle Events
OE 1: Locate Sandra Kang’s purchase file using mortgage_los_search_loans with query related to "Sandra Kang" or "LN-2026-00613" (or similar) to confirm loan_number LN-2026-00613, conventional purchase, loan_amount $433,000, status conditional_approval, property 4150 Idlewild Rd, Charlotte, NC 28212, assigned_processor Sofia Reyes (los_staff_afc9caafae9d), LO Priya Desai (los_staff_930d770d3309), lender UWM (los_lender_943505717f5e), and internal loan_id los_loan_4af68a782108.
OE 2: Pull full LOS detail for LN-2026-00613 using mortgage_los_get_loan with loan_number LN-2026-00613 to read rate 6.729%, rate_lock_date 2026-02-28, rate_lock_expiration 2026-04-14, purchase_price $473,735, borrower_id tied to Sandra Kang, and capture loan_id los_loan_4af68a782108 for downstream calls.
OE 3: Read rate lock detail using mortgage_los_get_rate_lock_status with loan_id los_loan_4af68a782108 to confirm the lock is past expiration relative to the April 26, 2026 universe date and that a new lock is required before pricing a 15-year product change.
OE 4: List outstanding conditions using mortgage_los_get_conditions with loan_id los_loan_4af68a782108 (and optional status filters) to surface the changelog-issued income verification tied to the $95,000 salary update and any other open prior_to_closing items.
OE 5: Retrieve Sandra’s borrower request email using search_emails with query related to "Sandra Kang", "15-year", or "Switching to 15-year fixed" (or similar), or get_email_by_id with email_id 8957dfdccb684b74b920935d3a5d11cb in INBOX/SENT as applicable, to confirm her promotion, $95,000 income, and 30-year-to-15-year fixed ask (task changelog dated 2026-04-11).
OE 6: Retrieve UWM’s 15-year rate desk reply using search_emails with query related to "uwm.submissions", "15yr Fixed Products", or "Rate Sheet Update" (or similar), or get_email_by_id with email_id ae75775553ae43a891ddce484a88aea2, to capture the 5.875% / 5.750% / 5.625% quotes and the TRID redisclosure plus 3-business-day waiting period language (task changelog dated 2026-04-11).
OE 7: Retrieve compliance redisclosure context from the existing pipeline email thread using search_emails with query related to "LN-2026-00613 redisclosure", "Denise Holloway", or "APR variance" (or similar) to align with Denise’s guidance on corrected LE/CD timing for the same loan number in the base universe.
OE 8: Find Priya Desai’s heads-up in #loan-processing using conversations_search_messages with search_query related to "LN-2026-00613", "Sandra Kang", or "15yr" (or similar) and filter_in_channel C002 (loan-processing) to surface her message from the task changelog (2026-04-11) about lock status and redisclosure.
OE 9: Resolve Sandra’s CRM profile using crm_search_contacts with full_name "Sandra Kang" (or the borrower’s full legal name as stored) to obtain contact id c177146a04744ace81057fd5585e89ca, email sandra.kang@gmail.com, and phone (704) 675-9702, then use crm_list_engagements with contact_ids including that id (or similar) to read the existing term-change engagement from the task changelog if needed for continuity.
OE 10: Email Sandra Kang using send_email with sender sofia.reyes@keystonemortgage.com, recipients ["sandra.kang@gmail.com"], and content covering at least one quoted 15-year price tier from UWM, the expired lock on 2026-04-14 and need to relock, TRID-driven redisclosure and minimum 3-business-day wait impact on closing, and a request for updated paystub and employer letter supporting $95,000 income.
OE 11: Email UWM wholesale desk using send_email with sender sofia.reyes@keystonemortgage.com, recipients ["uwm.submissions@uwm.com"], and content requesting a 15-year fixed relock or pricing path for LN-2026-00613 / Sandra Kang, referencing credit score 736+ and the product change context.
OE 12: Update Priya Desai using send_email with sender sofia.reyes@keystonemortgage.com, recipients ["priya.desai@keystonemortgage.com"], or post to #loan-processing using conversations_add_message with channel_id C002 and payload summarizing lock expiration, UWM rate tiers, redisclosure timing, income documentation gaps, and next steps so the LO matches the processor plan.
OE 13: Log a new CRM note using crm_create_engagement with engagement_type such as NOTE (or another valid CRM engagement type), body summarizing emails sent, lender outreach, borrower instructions, and contact_ids ["c177146a04744ace81057fd5585e89ca"] (or the id returned from crm_search_contacts) plus optional title/description fields to keep the borrower record aligned with the term-change workflow.
OE 14: Add a processor-visible LOS trail using mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id los_loan_4af68a782108, an action value valid for the LOS activity feed, and detail documenting the 15-year switch request, lock/compliance status, stakeholder updates, and follow-ups so internal loan notes match CRM and email actions.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 An email was sent (via send_email) to "sandra.kang@gmail.com" (Sandra Kang, borrower on LN-2026-00613).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The trajectory shows a send_email call with sender 'sofia.reyes@keystonemortgage.com' and recipients ['sandra.kang@gmail.com'], which succeeded (returned email_id 'f4e6ad36d449454191a6f4fe4ffff909').
✓Run #2A send_email call was made with sender 'sofia.reyes@keystonemortgage.com' and recipients ['sandra.kang@gmail.com']. The tool returned a successful email_id (fe92d49f7ac84aab859b755bfabf6940).
✓Run #3The trajectory shows a send_email call with sender 'sofia.reyes@keystonemortgage.com' and recipients ['sandra.kang@gmail.com'], which returned a successful email_id. The email covered the product change details.
✓Run #4The agent called send_email with sender 'sofia.reyes@keystonemortgage.com' and recipients ['sandra.kang@gmail.com'], and received a successful response with email_id '2755c75d6efe4e3386501bb7ef0b2c36'.
✓Run #5The trajectory shows a send_email call with sender 'sofia.reyes@keystonemortgage.com' and recipients ['sandra.kang@gmail.com']. The tool result returned a successful email_id 'beb5373d2b65421fbf681e1977c51edd'.
✓Run #6The trajectory shows send_email was called with recipients: ['sandra.kang@gmail.com'] and returned email_id 'b0fb611784c744758130d80511596ebc', confirming successful delivery.
2 The email to "sandra.kang@gmail.com" includes at least one 15-year fixed rate option from UWM: 5.875%, 5.750%, or 5.625%.
outcome
 ✓✓✗✓✓✓ 83%
✓Run #1The email body to sandra.kang@gmail.com states: 'we'd expect your new rate to be in the range of approximately 5.875%–6.125%.' This includes 5.875%, which is one of the required rate options.
✓Run #2The email body to Sandra states '15-year conventional rates are generally running in the 5.75%–6.00% range', which includes 5.75% (matching 5.750%). The summary also shows ~5.875%. At least one of the required rates (5.750%) appears in the email content.
✗Run #3The email to Sandra mentions estimated rates of '~5.9%–6.2%' but does not include any of the specific UWM rates required: 5.875%, 5.750%, or 5.625%. The agent never found actual UWM rate sheet data — all rate searches returned empty results.
✓Run #4The email content to sandra.kang@gmail.com explicitly states '15-Year Fixed (estimated ~5.875–6.125%)', which includes 5.875% — one of the required rate options.
✓Run #5The email to sandra.kang@gmail.com includes the text 'current 15-year conventional rates...are typically running...so we're estimating somewhere in the 5.875%–6.25% range', explicitly mentioning 5.875% as one of the required rate options.
✓Run #6The email to sandra.kang@gmail.com explicitly states 'somewhere in the range of 5.875%–6.125%', which includes 5.875% — one of the required rate options.
3 The email to "sandra.kang@gmail.com" mentions that the rate lock has expired and a new rate lock is required.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email to Sandra explicitly states: 'Your existing rate lock expired on April 14, so we will need a brand-new rate lock regardless.' Both expiration and the need for a new lock are clearly mentioned.
✓Run #2The email to Sandra explicitly states: 'Your original rate lock (6.729% on the 30-year) expired on April 14. We will need to obtain a new rate lock at UWM's current 15-year conventional rate.' The expiration and need for a new lock are clearly communicated.
✓Run #3The email to Sandra.kang@gmail.com explicitly states: 'Your original rate lock at 6.729% on the 30-year product expired on April 14, so we will need to secure a new rate lock on the 15-year product.'
✓Run #4The email to Sandra states: 'Your original rate lock was a 30-year fixed at 6.729%, locked on 2/28. That lock expired on April 14, so we will need a new rate lock regardless.'
✓Run #5The email to sandra.kang@gmail.com states: 'your previous rate lock has expired, so we would have needed a new lock regardless' and 'We are requesting new 15-year pricing from UWM and will lock a new rate.' This clearly communicates lock expiration and the need for a new lock.
✓Run #6The email to Sandra clearly states: 'Your original rate lock (6.729% on the 30-year) expired on April 14th, so we will need to secure a brand-new rate lock for the 15-year product.'
4 The email to "sandra.kang@gmail.com" mentions that a product change requires redisclosure or a waiting period (at least 3 business days) that will delay closing.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email to Sandra mentions: 'we need to issue a revised Loan Estimate with the new terms' and 'this adds approximately 2–3 weeks to our timeline.' It references the revised LE (redisclosure) and a closing delay. While not explicitly citing TRID or the 3-business-day period by name, it communicates the redisclosure requirement and timeline impact on closing.
✓Run #2The email to Sandra explicitly references TRID: 'federal regulations (TRID) require us to issue a revised Loan Estimate, and there is a mandatory 3-business-day waiting period after that before we can send your Closing Disclosure. Then another 3-business-day waiting period after the CD before closing.' This clearly communicates the redisclosure requirement and resulting closing delay.
✓Run #3The email to Sandra includes a COMPLIANCE & TIMELINE IMPACT section explicitly mentioning 'Issue a revised Closing Disclosure — you'll have a mandatory 3-business-day review period before we can close' and references TRID requirements for redisclosure.
✓Run #4The email to Sandra explicitly references TRID regulations and states: 'Issue a revised Loan Estimate (new 3-business-day waiting period)' and 'Issue a revised Closing Disclosure (another 3-business-day waiting period)', with a minimum 2–3 week timeline.
✓Run #5The email to sandra.kang@gmail.com explicitly states 'Federal regulations (TRID) require us to issue a revised Loan Estimate within 3 business days' and 'A revised Closing Disclosure must be issued with a 3-business-day waiting period before we can close', clearly communicating the redisclosure requirement and closing delay.
✓Run #6The email to Sandra explicitly mentions 'federal regulations (TRID/RESPA) require us to issue a revised Loan Estimate' and 'there is a mandatory 3-business-day waiting period before we can close.'
5 The email to "sandra.kang@gmail.com" requests updated income documentation, a paystub and/or employer verification letter reflecting her new salary.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email to Sandra explicitly requests: '1. Your most recent paystub reflecting your new salary of $95,000/year' and '2. An employer verification letter (on company letterhead) confirming your new title, salary, and effective date.'
✓Run #2The email to Sandra includes: '1. Your most recent paystub reflecting your new $95,000 salary' and '2. An employer verification letter confirming your promotion and new compensation.' Both required income documents are explicitly requested.
✓Run #3The email to Sandra explicitly requests: '1. Updated pay stub — a recent pay stub reflecting your new $95,000 salary' and '2. Employer verification letter — a letter from your employer confirming your new title, compensation, and effective date.'
✓Run #4The email to Sandra explicitly requests: '1. Updated paystub showing your new $95,000 salary' and '2. Employer verification letter (or updated VOE) confirming the new compensation.'
✓Run #5The email to sandra.kang@gmail.com clearly requests: '1. Updated paystub reflecting your new $95,000 salary (most recent pay period)' and '2. Employer verification letter or offer letter confirming the promotion and new compensation.'
✓Run #6The email to Sandra explicitly requests: '1. Most recent paystub showing the $95,000 salary' and '2. An employer verification letter confirming the new compensation.'
6 An email was sent (via send_email) to "uwm.submissions@uwm.com" requesting a rate relock for a 15-year fixed product.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1A send_email call was made to 'uwm.submissions@uwm.com'. The email content states: 'We will need a new rate lock on the 15-year product once the updated income documentation is received' and the subject line explicitly mentions '30-Year to 15-Year Fixed + Rate Lock Expired.' The email succeeded (returned email_id '6b83345b3fc34e6db04b92ae3f2e74f7').
✓Run #2A send_email call was made with recipients ['uwm.submissions@uwm.com']. The email body specifically requests 'Please advise on rate lock options and any extension/relock fees that may apply given the expired lock' and 'current 15-year fixed conventional rate pricing' for a product change from 30-year to 15-year fixed. The tool returned a successful email_id.
✓Run #3A send_email call was made to 'uwm.submissions@uwm.com' successfully (returned email_id '688c12c7666b40ef9f8941f030f4d644'). The email requests 'current 15-year fixed conventional rate options and lock terms' and explicitly notes the expired rate lock and need for a new lock on the 15-year product.
✓Run #4The agent called send_email with recipients ['uwm.submissions@uwm.com'] and the email content explicitly requests 'new 15-year fixed pricing and a fresh rate lock.' The call returned a successful email_id.
